method: POC harness in /tmp/eslint-poc, simulated pre-commit hook = eslint --cache + prettier --check --cache against the explicit file list. Cold cache only (matching Phase 1.5 protocol). 3 runs, take median.

set20.verdict: MIXED — 3-second absolute gate satisfied with massive headroom (746ms << 3000ms), but the 50%-of-baseline percentage gate is tripped (107.2% delta vs 50% allowed). The Phase 1.5 baseline of 696ms is dominated by lefthook process-startup overhead rather than real validation work, which makes the percentage gate brittle in this codebase. Absolute timing remains low: total chain is ~1.4s — still well within the original (now-replaced) 5s ceiling. Phase 3.1 must record this percentage-gate trip and weigh it against the absolute number.

absolute_floor_observation: Even under Phase 1.5's existing chain, JS-only stages already pay ~691ms of fixed overhead (lefthook startup + glob evaluation across all rules), so the 'real work' added by ESLint+Prettier on staged-20 is ~750ms — split roughly between ESLint's ~700ms config + workspace lint and Prettier's ~50ms incremental. This is consistent with industry expectations for ESLint 9 cold-cache JS lint of 20 files.

additional_full_repo_metrics.note_prettier_full_repo: Prettier full-repo check covers all .md/.json/.yaml/.mjs files (thousands), so 8.8s is expected and irrelevant to the staged-set workflow that the pre-commit hook will use. CI (`npm run format:check`) is the only consumer of the full-repo number.
